name: San Francisco Firehouse Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve yeast in 1/2 cup warm water (110°F). Let stand until foamy.
2. Cook onion in hot oil until tender.
3. Combine onion, milk, parsley, sugar, salt, dillweed, and sage.
4. Stir in yeast.
5. Mix in cornmeal.
6. Beat in half the flour.
7. Add remaining flour by hand to form a moderately soft dough.
8. Turn out onto lightly floured surface.
9. Knead 2 to 5 minutes.
10. Place in greased bowl, turning once to grease the surface.
11. Cover and let rise until doubled, about 1 hour.
12. Punch down; divide into two portions.
13. Shape each portion into a loaf and place in well-greased 16-ounce coffee cans.
14. Cover and let rise until doubled, 20 to 45 minutes.
15. Bake at 350°F for 45 minutes, loosely covering with foil during the last 15 minutes.
